mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns exceed 60% when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns in more than 40% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in regime-specific subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and year-over-year inflation exceeding 3.0% creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust macroeconomic expansion and corporate earnings momentum, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative returns in more than 60% of cases or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production coinciding with declining retail sales signals a synchronized supply-demand breakdown that forces corporate earnings downgrades and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative supply-demand signals are followed by positive returns in more than 55% of cases or the joint effect fails to achieve statistical significance in regime-specific tests.
